title: Water Fill Timeout
shortDescription: The dishwasher has not filled with water within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error i10 on Zanussi dishwashers indicates the machine did not reach the required water level within the timeout. Zanussi dishwashers use the Electrolux iXX error code system. Usually a water supply issue.

fixSteps.title: Check water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure tap is fully open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect inlet hose and filter
fixSteps.instruction: Check for kinks. Clean mesh filter screens.

fixSteps.title: Test inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Check for voltage during fill. If voltage present but no water, valve faulty.

fixSteps.title: Replace inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off water, disconnect hose, swap valve.

videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i10 water fill error fix

faq.q: Are Zanussi dishwasher codes the same as Electrolux?
faq.a: Yes, they share the same iXX error code platform.

faq.q: Can low pressure cause i10?
faq.a: Yes, minimum 1 bar pressure is needed.

faq.q: How do I clean the filter?
faq.a: Disconnect the hose and pull the mesh screen out with pliers.

title: Drain Timeout Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher could not drain within the allowed time.
fullDescription: Error i20 indicates the drain cycle did not complete. Water remains in the sump. Caused by filter blockages, drain hose issues, or failed pump.

fixSteps.title: Clean drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean all filter components thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for kinks and blockages. Ensure high loop.

fixSteps.title: Inspect pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: Check for obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Replace drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: If motor failed, replace.

videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i20 drain blocked fix

faq.q: How often clean the filter?
faq.a: Every 3-5 cycles.

faq.q: Can the sink cause i20?
faq.a: Yes, a blocked waste pipe prevents drainage.

faq.q: Why the high loop?
faq.a: Prevents siphoning.

title: Anti-Flood / Water Leak Detected
shortDescription: Water has been detected in the base tray.
fullDescription: Error i30 indicates the float switch detected water leaking into the base. The machine stops filling and runs the drain pump to prevent flooding.

fixSteps.title: Tilt and drain
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug, turn off water. Tilt machine to drain base.

fixSteps.title: Inspect door seal
fixSteps.instruction: Check for tears or gaps.

fixSteps.title: Check hoses
fixSteps.instruction: Remove panels, inspect all connections.

fixSteps.title: Find and fix leak
fixSteps.instruction: Run short cycle while watching for leak source.

whenToCallPro: If leak involves pump or sump.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i30 anti flood leak fix

faq.q: Can too much detergent cause i30?
faq.a: Yes, suds can overflow through seals.

faq.q: Can I just dry the base?
faq.a: Temporarily, but the leak will recur.

faq.q: How does the float switch work?
faq.a: A polystyrene float rises with water and triggers a microswitch.

title: Pressure Sensor / Water Level Fault
shortDescription: The water level pressure sensor is providing incorrect readings.
fullDescription: Error i40 indicates the pressure sensor or analogue water level sensor is reading outside expected range. The dishwasher cannot determine the correct water level for filling and draining.

fixSteps.title: Check pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Find the small hose from sump to sensor. Ensure connected and not blocked.

fixSteps.title: Clean pressure chamber
fixSteps.instruction: Clean any buildup where the hose connects to the sump.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test sensor output according to service manual.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If faulty, replace the pressure sensor.

whenToCallPro: If sensor and hose test fine.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i40 pressure sensor fix

faq.q: What is the pressure sensor?
faq.a: It measures water level by sensing air pressure in a tube connected to the sump.

faq.q: Can detergent buildup cause i40?
faq.a: Yes, residue can block the pressure hose.

faq.q: Is i40 common?
faq.a: Less common than drain or fill errors, but straightforward to fix.

title: Circulation Pump Triac Fault
shortDescription: The triac controlling the circulation pump has failed on the control board.
fullDescription: Error i50 indicates the triac semiconductor that controls the wash circulation pump has short-circuited or failed. The pump may not run or may run uncontrolled.
symptoms: Pump runs continuously or not at all

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test circulation pump
fixSteps.instruction: Check pump motor resistance and insulation.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board
fixSteps.instruction: Look for burnt or discolored triac on board.

fixSteps.title: Replace control board
fixSteps.instruction: If triac is damaged, replace the board. Also replace pump if it caused the failure.

whenToCallPro: i50 requires board replacement. Professional recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i50 pump triac board fix

faq.q: Can the triac be replaced alone?
faq.a: With skilled soldering, but board replacement is standard.

faq.q: Will a surge protector help?
faq.a: Yes, protects against voltage spikes.

faq.q: Is i50 common?
faq.a: Less common but typically requires board replacement.

title: Heating Element / Temperature Fault
shortDescription: The heating element or temperature sensor circuit has a fault.
fullDescription: Error i60 indicates a fault in the water heating system — the element, NTC sensor, or heater circuit on the control board. Water is not being heated correctly.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test heater
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance (15-30 ohms) and insulation (over 2 megaohms).

fixSteps.title: Test NTC
fixSteps.instruction: Check NTC resistance (10K-50K at room temp).

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty component
fixSteps.instruction: Replace heater, NTC, or both as needed.

whenToCallPro: If heater trips the RCD or board relay suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i60 heater temperature fix

faq.q: Can I wash cold?
faq.a: Machine will run but cleaning very poor.

faq.q: Why does RCD trip?
faq.a: Heater leaking to ground trips safety switch.

faq.q: Is the NTC expensive?
faq.a: No, typically 5-15.

title: NTC Heater Short / Open Circuit
shortDescription: The NTC sensor or heater has an open or short circuit condition.
fullDescription: Error i70 specifically indicates the NTC sensor circuit is open or shorted. This is more specific than i60 and points directly to the sensor or its wiring rather than the heater element itself.

fixSteps.title: Test NTC
fixSteps.instruction: Locate NTC in sump. Disconnect and measure resistance.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect NTC wiring and connectors for damage or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Clean connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Clean any corrosion with electronics contact cleaner.

fixSteps.title: Replace NTC
fixSteps.instruction: If failed, swap the sensor.

whenToCallPro: Only if NTC and wiring are fine — board fault.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i70 NTC sensor fix

faq.q: What is the difference between i60 and i70?
faq.a: i60 covers the whole heating system. i70 is specifically the NTC sensor circuit.

faq.q: Is the NTC easy to replace?
faq.a: Yes, it is a simple pull-out/push-in sensor.

faq.q: Can I buy generic NTC sensors?
faq.a: Yes, as long as the resistance value matches the original.

title: EEPROM / Communication Error
shortDescription: The EEPROM memory or board communication has a fault.
fullDescription: Error i80 indicates the EEPROM memory on the control board has corrupted data or there is a communication fault between electronic modules. The dishwasher cannot execute programs correctly.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reseat connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Check and reseat all board connectors.

fixSteps.title: EEPROM reprogramming
fixSteps.instruction: Technician may reprogram with correct firmware.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: If reprogramming fails, replace the board.

whenToCallPro: i80 almost always requires professional intervention.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i80 EEPROM board error fix

faq.q: Can a power cut cause i80?
faq.a: Yes, especially during a cycle.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing?
faq.a: A board at 70-170 is worthwhile on machines under 8-10 years.

faq.q: Can I buy a pre-programmed board?
faq.a: Yes, order the exact part for your model.

title: Processor / Configuration Error
shortDescription: The main processor has a configuration or firmware error.
fullDescription: Error i90 indicates the main processor on the control board has a critical configuration error. The firmware may be corrupted or the processor has failed. The dishwasher cannot operate.

fixSteps.title: Full power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug for 30 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure correct mains voltage. Try different outlet.

fixSteps.title: Inspect board
fixSteps.instruction: Check for burnt components or swollen capacitors.

fixSteps.title: Replace board
fixSteps.instruction: Install pre-programmed replacement board.

whenToCallPro: i90 is a board-level fault. Professional replacement needed.
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher i90 processor error board replacement

faq.q: Is i90 repairable?
faq.a: Only by replacing the control board.

faq.q: Is the machine worth fixing?
faq.a: A board at 70-170 plus labor is usually worthwhile on newer machines.

faq.q: Do I need the exact board?
faq.a: Yes, the board must match your model for correct firmware.

title: Spray Arm Rotation Blocked
shortDescription: The spray arm rotation sensor detects the spray arm is not turning.
fullDescription: Error iA0 indicates the spray arm monitoring sensor has detected the arms are not rotating during the wash cycle. This can be caused by physical obstruction, pump pressure issues, or sensor faults.

causes.cause: Dishes or utensils blocking spray arm rotation

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the door and spin the spray arms by hand. Ensure no dishes, handles, or utensils are blocking rotation.

fixSteps.title: Clean spray arms
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the spray arms and clear all holes of debris. Check bearings for wear.

fixSteps.title: Rearrange dishes
fixSteps.instruction: Reload the dishwasher ensuring no items protrude below or above racks into the spray arm path.

fixSteps.title: Check pump and sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If arms spin freely but still get iA0, check the circulation pump and the spray arm sensor.

whenToCallPro: Only if spray arms spin freely but the error persists (sensor or pump fault).
videoSearchQuery: Zanussi dishwasher iA0 spray arm blocked fix

faq.q: Is iA0 usually a loading issue?
faq.a: Yes, in most cases it is caused by dishes or utensils blocking the spray arm.

faq.q: How should I load to prevent iA0?
faq.a: Ensure tall items do not extend below the racks, and that all items are stable. Spin the spray arms by hand after loading.

faq.q: Do all Zanussi dishwashers have spray arm sensors?
faq.a: Not all models. Budget models may not monitor spray arm rotation.
